source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/Univariate/CompetingRiskAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- D6 EMITTER-VERIFIED exact oracle. CompetingRiskAnalysis drives a Bayesian DEMCzs MCMC over a Gumbel+Weibull CompetingRisksModel and assembles the frequency (mode) curve + credible band on the exceedance grid. The values below are the exact output of the real RMC.BestFit CompetingRiskAnalysis driven by tools/oracle_emitter (BuildAndRunAnalysis mirrors competing_risk_analysis::run(): drive the seeded BayesianAnalysis serially, then CreateFrequencyAnalysisResults); the seeded DEMCzs chain reproduces the ported C++ core to ~1e-10 relative. Values also reproduce bit-identically across C++/R/Python (shared compiled core + bit-exact Mersenne Twister). thinning_interval pinned to 1. Short run (iterations 100, output_length 400). Parameter layout [Gumbel location, Gumbel scale, Weibull scale, Weibull shape]; rmse over the competing-risks inverse-CDF grid can be non-finite and is NOT asserted. Tolerance held at rel 1e-5 to absorb cross-platform MCMC float variation (the seeded chain runs many post-guard iterations; libm last-bit differences can accumulate across OSes).
